# UC-11.6.2 · Proofpoint MTA Business Email Compromise and Spoofing Telemetry

## Description

MTA logs capture authentication outcomes on inbound mail—failures and suspicious subjects often precede BEC payloads that TAP may score later. Surfacing high-volume failing senders complements TAP impostor scores with protocol-level evidence.

## Value

Accelerates BEC triage for messaging teams and supplies forensic context for vendor impersonation and payroll fraud attempts.

## Implementation

(1) Enable detailed MTA logging to Splunk. (2) Tune subject regex for your industry. (3) Allow-list bulk marketing domains via lookup. (4) Correlate with `proofpoint_tap_siem` on `message_id`. (5) Route high counts to fraud operations.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:mta" earliest=-24h
| eval from_addr=coalesce(sender, Sender, mailfrom, "")
| eval auth=coalesce(authentication_results, Authentication_Results, spf_dkim, "")
| eval subj=coalesce(subject, Subject, "")
| where match(lower(auth), "(?i)fail|softfail|dmarc.fail|spf.fail|compauth.*fail") OR match(lower(subj), "(?i)wire|invoice|urgent.*payment|ceo|gift card)")
| eval pps=coalesce(pp_message_id, message_id, MessageID, "")
| stats count values(subj) as subjects by from_addr auth
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (sender, auth summary, count), Sankey to internal recipients if field exists, timeline.
